title: Unit Testing Practice
instructions: Write test functions to verify that the given functions work correctly. Test normal cases, edge cases, and error conditions.
solution:
def is_even(n):
    """Return True if n is even"""
    return n % 2 == 0

def get_first_element(items):
    """Return first element of list"""
    if not items:
        raise ValueError("List is empty")
    return items[0]

def test_is_even():
    """Test is_even function"""
    # Test positive even numbers
    assert is_even(2) == True
    assert is_even(4) == True
    
    # Test positive odd numbers
    assert is_even(1) == False
    assert is_even(3) == False
    
    # Test zero
    assert is_even(0) == True
    
    # Test negative numbers
    assert is_even(-2) == True
    assert is_even(-3) == False

def test_get_first_element():
    """Test get_first_element function"""
    # Test normal case
    assert get_first_element([1, 2, 3]) == 1
    assert get_first_element(["a", "b"]) == "a"
    
    # Test single element
    assert get_first_element([5]) == 5
    
    # Test empty list raises error
    try:
        get_first_element([])
        assert False, "Should have raised ValueError"
    except ValueError as e:
        assert str(e) == "List is empty"
hints: Test multiple scenarios: positive, negative, zero, edge cases
Use assert statements to check conditions
Use try-except to test that exceptions are raised correctly

testCases.description: test_is_even should verify that even numbers return True
testCases.validation: assert is_even(2) == True and is_even(4) == True and is_even(0) == True, 'Even numbers and zero should return True'

testCases.description: test_is_even should verify that odd numbers return False
testCases.validation: assert is_even(1) == False and is_even(3) == False and is_even(7) == False, 'Odd numbers should return False'

testCases.description: get_first_element should return the first item of a non-empty list
testCases.validation: assert get_first_element([10, 20, 30]) == 10, 'Should return first element of list'

testCases.description: get_first_element should raise ValueError for empty list
is_even should handle negative even numbers correctly
testCases.validation: assert is_even(-2) == True and is_even(-4) == True, 'Negative even numbers should return True'

testCases.description: get_first_element should work with single-element lists
testCases.validation: assert get_first_element([99]) == 99, 'Should return the only element in a single-element list'

description: Practice writing unit tests using Python assert statements. Write test functions that verify correct behavior for normal cases, edge cases like zero and negative numbers, and error conditions like empty lists. Learn to test that functions raise expected exceptions using try-except patterns.
